Title: Re: Huntress Low Incident | LOW - Incident on AAOC-28 (AOC - Apartment Association of Orange County)
Notes:
We received a security alert indicating that an unencrypted password file exists on system AAOC-28 at the following path:
C:\Users\cordero.AAOC\Apartment Association of Orange County\Secure - Documents\_ACCOUNTING\_PASSWORDS\Accounting Passwords 12-18-2023.xlsx.

This file contains passwords and is stored in an unencrypted state, posing a significant security risk. Such files are vulnerable to unauthorized access.

Actions Taken:

  1. The file path was confirmed and flagged as a security concern.

  2. We advised Cordero to take one of the following actions:

    • Encrypt the file with a strong password via File > Protect Using Password or Tools > Protect > Encrypt > Encrypt with Password.

    • If the file is no longer needed, delete it to prevent potential security risks.

Next Steps:

  • Follow-up with Cordero to ensure encryption is applied, or confirm deletion of the file.

Status: Awaiting confirmation from Cordero on encryption or file deletion.

Title: Huntress Low Incident | LOW - Incident on AAOC-20 (AOC - Apartment Association of Orange County)
Notes:
We received a security alert indicating that the following file exists on system AAOC-20:

C:\Users\Van.AAOC\Apartment Association of Orange County\Secure - Documents\_ACCOUNTING\_PASSWORDS\Accounting Passwords 12-18-2023.xlsx

The file stores passwords and is not encrypted, which poses a significant security risk. Unencrypted files containing sensitive information are vulnerable if attackers gain access to the system.

Actions Taken:

  1. We advised Van to take one of the following actions:

    • Encrypt the file using a strong password by going to File > Protect Using Password (or Tools > Protect > Encrypt > Encrypt with Password).

    • If the file is no longer needed, delete it to avoid security risks.

Next Steps:

  • Await confirmation from Van regarding the encryption or deletion of the file.

  • Ensure that any future password-protected files are encrypted to prevent security vulnerabilities.

Status: Awaiting confirmation from the user.
